title: REFUGEE SERVICES - 211info
text: OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES REFUGEE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps refugees and others with an eligible immigration status get help with cash, medical, employment, and other services when they first come to Oregon. Oregon gets funding to provide these services from the federal Office of Refugee Resettlement and State Legislature. Local resettlement agencies help refugees get connected to services. 500 Summer Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: -Refugees and others with an eligible immigration status who are age 18 or older, single or legally married with no children, and not enrolled as full-time college students -Have been in the US for less than one year as a refugee or received a qualifying status within the past year, and meet income guidelines Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (800) 699-9075 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (800) 699-9075 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Apply online, by phone, or request a paper application in person at a local office FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Medicaid * Refugees/Entrants/Asylees Refugee/Entrant Cash Assistance Other Services Offered At This Location: DISASTER SERVICES - ODHS DISASTER CASE MANAGEMENT HOTLINE EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - YOUTH EXPERIENCING HOMELESSNESS PROGRAM INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - ODHS LANGUAGE ACCESS AND ACCOMMODATIONS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - ODHS LOCAL OFFICES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SAFE ABUSE REPORTING HOTLINE LAW ENFORCEMENT SERVICES - ODHS FRAUD INVESTIGATIONS PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - OREGON ONE ELIGIBILITY SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - CHILDREN'S EXTRAORDINARY NEEDS PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES
